spec_match.rationale: Authors a gradient with luminance > 1.0, rendered into a float16 canvas — captures the wider-than-sdr range the spec adds.

interactive.rationale: Five inputs for stops + a button to add stops, live re-render.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb describes a luminance > 1.0 gradient on a float16 vs unorm8 canvas; the page does that.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't show what the same gradient looks like clipped to unorm8 — the comparison is implied but not rendered. Also missing P3/rec2020 colour-space toggle.

references.rationale: The page now includes a see-also section with the ChromeStatus feature 5086141338877952 and the HTML canvas spec.

design_system.rationale: Page styling uses variables; default stop colours are raw `#ff4400` and `#0044ff` in JS state. Those are content (the user picks colours) so acceptable, but ensure the inputs use the design system colour picker styling.

openQuestions.title: Render both canvases side-by-side
openQuestions.detail: Mention of unorm8 vs float16 is in the prose but only one canvas paints. Add the unorm8 lane so the clipping difference is visible.

openQuestions.title: Toggle colorSpace: srgb / display-p3 / rec2020
openQuestions.detail: Float16 + display-p3 / rec2020 is where the spec really opens up. Add a colour-space select so authors see the gamut difference, not just the luminance.

summary: Promising HDR-authoring tool; it still needs a side-by-side unorm8 baseline canvas and color-space toggle to fully show the spec impact.
